canonical_definition: The ability to interpret and analyze three-dimensional spatial information from visual inputs, often aided by depth data.

definitions.text: The ability of a model to process and comprehend information from both language and 3D visual data, such as point clouds or RGB-D scans, to understand scenes in the physical world.
